Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - The frontline reach problem most internal comms teams haven't solved yet
Description:
Topic: The structural reach problem on frontline / deskless workforces — why email-only IC misses most of the company
Post type: static-theme
Wraps: internal-communications-in-manufacturing
Copy:
[CAPTION]
A typical mid-market manufacturer has 5,000 employees.

70 to 90 percent of them work on the plant floor. They don't have corporate email. They don't log into SharePoint. They aren't on Teams.

So when the internal comms team reports a 60 percent open rate on the latest safety bulletin, what does that actually mean? If the office is 20 percent of headcount, the real reach against the total workforce is closer to 12 percent.

That's the frontline reach problem most internal comms teams haven't named, let alone solved.

Gallup's 2026 State of the Global Workplace report puts on-site, non-remote-capable engagement at 17 percent. That's 13 percentage points behind exclusively-remote workers, and the widest cohort split in the report.

A program that defaults to email-first measurement is reporting on the office and calling it the company.

Full breakdown ↓

(Link in comments.)

[ASSET SPEC]
Static graphic — stat callout card. Two stacked stats with a connecting line: 60% (reported open rate) -> 12% (actual reach against total headcount). Subhead: "The frontline reach problem". Cerkl primary palette, bold sans on numbers, square 1:1, logo bottom-right. Alt: pull-quote card — "A program that defaults to email-first measurement is reporting on the office and calling it the company."

Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - Link post: Internal Communications in Manufacturing
Description:
Topic: Link post pointing to the manufacturing playbook blog
Post type: static-blog
Wraps: internal-communications-in-manufacturing
Copy:
[CAPTION]
Manufacturing internal comms has its own constraints.

The workforce is 70 to 90 percent frontline, working across 2 or 3 shifts at 10 to 20 sites. Most don't have corporate email. The audit trail for safety messaging carries OSHA and insurance consequences. The corporate intranet built at headquarters is, for the plant floor, a website they never visit.

Our 2026 frontline playbook covers the 4 dimensions that make manufacturing IC structurally different, the 5-channel stack that replaces email-first delivery, and a 30-day plan any IC lead can run before the budget conversation.

Summary: Social Media - LinkedIn - What percentage of your workforce reads every important company update
Description:
Topic: LinkedIn poll on cross-channel read-coverage self-assessment
Post type: poll
Wraps: cross-channel-read-gap
Copy:
[CAPTION]
Internal comms leaders, here's a question worth sitting with.

When leadership asks "did this land?", you probably reach for an open rate or a page-view number. That number reflects the channel you can measure. The audience you need to reach lives somewhere else.

If you publish across email, Teams, mobile, and intranet but only report from one of those channels, you have a structural blind spot. As of 2026, no public IC engagement benchmark even measures across channels.

So the honest answer is rarely the one in the dashboard.

What's your gut estimate?

[POLL]
Question: What % of your workforce reads every important company update?
Options: Less than 30% | 30 to 50% | 50 to 80% | More than 80%
Duration: 1 week

[ASSET SPEC]
N/A — native LinkedIn poll widget, no design asset needed.
